sentiment.rationale: Israel is subjected to bitter bondage. The midwives' fear of God rather than the king provides a note of faithfulness amid oppression.
Moses is born and hidden, kills an Egyptian, and flees to Midian. God hears the groaning of His people in bondage.
God reveals Himself in the burning bush as I AM, commissioning Moses to deliver Israel.
God gives Moses signs and powers, though Moses resists the call. Aaron is appointed as spokesman.
Pharaoh increases the burdens on Israel after Moses and Aaron's first appeal, causing deep distress.
God reaffirms His covenant promises and His name Jehovah, renewing the commission to Moses.
The first plagues begin. Aaron's rod becomes a serpent. Water is turned to blood as judgment falls on Egypt.
Plagues of frogs, lice, and flies strike Egypt. Pharaoh's heart is hardened repeatedly.
Plagues of murrain, boils, and hail devastate Egypt while Israel is preserved.
Plagues of locusts and darkness intensify the judgments. Pharaoh threatens Moses with death.
The final plague is announced: the death of every firstborn in Egypt.
Institution of the Passover. The destroyer passes over Israel. The exodus from Egypt begins.
Consecration of the firstborn and the feast of unleavened bread. God leads Israel with a pillar of cloud and fire.
The miraculous crossing of the Red Sea. Egypt's army is destroyed. Israel sees the great work of the Lord.
The Song of Moses celebrates the triumph at the Red Sea. Miriam leads the women in song.
Israel murmurs for food. God provides manna and quail but tests their obedience in gathering.
Water from the rock at Horeb. Israel prevails over Amalek while Moses' hands are lifted.
Jethro counsels Moses to delegate judgment. A model of wise governance and peace.
Israel arrives at Sinai. God descends on the mount with thunder, lightning, and a thick cloud. Israel is offered a covenant as a kingdom of priests.
The Ten Commandments are given directly by God. The people fear and stand afar off.
Laws governing servants, personal injury, and restitution. A code of justice and accountability.
Laws of property, moral conduct, and social responsibility, with warnings against oppressing the vulnerable.
Laws of justice, the sabbatical year, three annual feasts, and the promise of an angel to guide Israel.
The covenant is ratified with blood. Moses, Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy elders see God. Moses ascends into the cloud.
Instructions for the Ark of the Covenant, the table of shewbread, and the golden candlestick. God will dwell among His people.
Detailed instructions for the tabernacle curtains, boards, and veil, creating a sacred dwelling place.
Instructions for the altar of burnt offering, the court of the tabernacle, and the oil for the lamp.
The priestly garments for Aaron are described in detail, including the ephod, breastplate, and holy crown.
The consecration ceremony for the priests. God promises to dwell among Israel and be their God.
The altar of incense, the atonement money, the laver, the anointing oil, and the holy incense.
Bezaleel and Aholiab are called and filled with the Spirit. The Sabbath is established as a perpetual sign.
The golden calf apostasy. Moses intercedes. God's wrath is kindled. Three thousand are slain.
Moses pleads for God's presence. God speaks to Moses face to face. Moses asks to see God's glory.
God proclaims His name and character: merciful, gracious, longsuffering. New tablets are given. Moses' face shines.
Moses assembles Israel. The people bring willing offerings for the tabernacle. Bezaleel and Aholiab are appointed.
The people give so abundantly they must be restrained. Construction of the tabernacle begins.
Bezaleel makes the Ark, the table, the candlestick, and the altar of incense.
The altar of burnt offering and the laver are made. An accounting of the materials is given.
The priestly garments are completed. All the work is done as the Lord commanded Moses.
The tabernacle is erected and anointed. The glory of the Lord fills the tabernacle. The cloud guides Israel.

missingCharacters.name: Shiphrah
missingCharacters.description: One of the two Hebrew midwives who feared God and refused Pharaoh's order to kill newborn Hebrew boys.

missingCharacters.name: Puah
missingCharacters.description: One of the two Hebrew midwives who feared God and defied Pharaoh's command to kill Hebrew male infants.

summaries.summary: The children of Israel multiply greatly in Egypt. A new Pharaoh enslaves them with harsh labor. Hebrew midwives Shiphrah and Puah defy his order to kill male babies.
Moses is born, hidden in a basket, and raised in Pharaoh's court. He kills an Egyptian, flees to Midian, marries Zipporah, and God hears Israel's groaning.
God appears to Moses in a burning bush on Horeb, reveals His name I AM THAT I AM, and commissions him to deliver Israel from Egypt.
God gives Moses three signs, appoints Aaron as his spokesman, and sends them to Egypt. Zipporah circumcises their son on the journey.
Moses and Aaron ask Pharaoh to let Israel go, but Pharaoh increases the workload by requiring them to gather their own straw for bricks.
God reaffirms His covenant with Israel, declaring His name Jehovah, and recommissions Moses and Aaron. The genealogies of Reuben, Simeon, and Levi are given.
Moses and Aaron confront Pharaoh. Aaron's rod becomes a serpent. The first plague turns the waters of Egypt to blood.
The plagues of frogs, lice, and flies strike Egypt. Pharaoh bargains with Moses but repeatedly hardens his heart.
The plagues of murrain on livestock, boils, and devastating hail fall on Egypt, while the land of Goshen is spared.
Plagues of locusts and thick darkness come upon Egypt. Pharaoh threatens Moses and refuses to let all of Israel depart.
Moses announces the final plague: at midnight the firstborn of Egypt will die. Israel is given favor in the sight of the Egyptians.
God institutes the Passover. The destroyer kills Egypt's firstborn. Israel is thrust out of Egypt after 430 years with great substance.
God commands the sanctification of the firstborn and the feast of unleavened bread. He leads Israel by a pillar of cloud by day and fire by night.
Pharaoh pursues Israel to the Red Sea. Moses parts the waters, Israel crosses on dry ground, and the Egyptian army is destroyed.
Moses and Israel sing a triumphant song celebrating their deliverance. Miriam leads the women. At Marah, bitter waters are made sweet.
Israel murmurs in the wilderness of Sin. God sends quail and manna, establishing the Sabbath rest and testing their obedience.
Water comes from the rock at Horeb when Moses strikes it. Israel defeats Amalek in battle while Aaron and Hur support Moses' hands.
Jethro visits Moses, praises God, and counsels Moses to appoint judges to share the burden of governing the people.
Israel camps at Sinai. God offers them a covenant as a kingdom of priests. He descends on the mount with thunder, lightning, and a thick cloud.
God speaks the Ten Commandments from Sinai. The people tremble and stand afar off. Moses draws near to the thick darkness where God is.
Laws are given concerning Hebrew servants, personal injuries, violence, and the principle of proportional justice.
Laws of restitution for theft and property damage, protections for the vulnerable, and commands regarding moral and religious obligations.
Laws of justice and mercy, the sabbatical year and Sabbath, three annual feasts, and the promise of an angel to lead Israel to Canaan.
The covenant is ratified with sacrificial blood. Moses, Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy elders behold God. Moses enters the cloud for forty days.
God commands an offering for the tabernacle and gives patterns for the Ark of the Covenant, the table of shewbread, and the golden candlestick.
Instructions are given for the tabernacle's curtains, boards, bars, and the veil separating the Holy Place from the Most Holy Place.
God gives instructions for the brazen altar, the court of the tabernacle, and the continual burning of the lamp with pure olive oil.
Holy garments are designed for Aaron and his sons, including the ephod, breastplate of judgment, robe, and golden plate inscribed HOLINESS TO THE LORD.
The consecration ceremony for Aaron and his sons as priests is prescribed, along with the daily burnt offerings and God's promise to dwell among Israel.
Instructions for the altar of incense, the atonement money, the brasen laver, the holy anointing oil, and the sacred incense.
Bezaleel and Aholiab are called and filled with the Spirit for tabernacle craftsmanship. The Sabbath is established as a sign between God and Israel.
While Moses is on Sinai, Israel makes a golden calf. Moses intercedes, breaks the tablets, confronts Aaron, and the Levites execute judgment.
God threatens to withdraw His presence. Moses pleads and is told he has found grace. God speaks to Moses face to face and shows His glory.
Moses receives new tablets. God proclaims His name: merciful, gracious, longsuffering. The covenant is renewed. Moses' face shines from the encounter.
Moses instructs Israel regarding the Sabbath and calls for willing offerings and skilled workers for the tabernacle construction.
The people bring more than enough offerings. Bezaleel and the craftsmen begin constructing the tabernacle's curtains, boards, and veil.
Bezaleel constructs the Ark of the Covenant, the table of shewbread, the golden candlestick, and the altar of incense.
The altar of burnt offering and the brasen laver are made. An inventory of gold, silver, and brass used in the tabernacle is recorded.
The priestly garments are completed exactly as the Lord commanded. Moses inspects all the finished work and blesses the workers.
The tabernacle is erected and anointed on the first day of the first month. The glory of the Lord fills the tabernacle with a cloud.

crossReferences.note: Jesus quotes this verse to prove the resurrection, identifying God as the God of the living.
Jesus echoes the divine I AM when He declares 'Before Abraham was, I am.'
The Passover lamb's unbroken bones foreshadow that none of Christ's bones were broken at the crucifixion.
Jesus identifies Himself as the true bread from heaven, fulfilling the type of manna.
Paul identifies the rock that gave water as Christ: 'that Rock was Christ.'
Peter applies the 'kingdom of priests and holy nation' language to the church.
Paul quotes the fifth commandment as the first commandment with a promise.
The blood of the covenant at Sinai is a type of the blood of the new covenant in Christ.
The author of Hebrews quotes the command to make all things according to the heavenly pattern.
Paul echoes Moses' willingness to be blotted out for the sake of his people.
John affirms that no man has seen God but that the Son has revealed Him.
The psalmist echoes God's self-proclaimed character: merciful, gracious, and slow to anger.

historicalContext.setting: Red Sea shore to Marah and Elim

verses.reason: The midwives feared God and did not obey Pharaoh's command to kill the male children.
God heard their groaning and remembered His covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
'Put off thy shoes from off thy feet, for the place whereon thou standest is holy ground.'
God reveals His name: 'I AM THAT I AM.'
God promises to be with Moses' mouth and teach him what to say.
God declares He appeared to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob as God Almighty but by His name JEHOVAH was not known to them.
God declares He raised up Pharaoh to show His power and declare His name in all the earth.
'When I see the blood, I will pass over you'—the foundational Passover promise.
The Lord went before them in a pillar of cloud by day and a pillar of fire by night.
'Fear ye not, stand still, and see the salvation of the LORD.'
'The LORD shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.'
'The LORD is my strength and song, and he is become my salvation.'
'Who is like unto thee, O LORD, among the gods?'
God promises to rain bread from heaven to prove the people.
God commands Moses to strike the rock and water comes forth for the people.
Jethro's counsel to select able men who fear God for rulers.
'If ye will obey my voice indeed, and keep my covenant, then ye shall be a peculiar treasure unto me.'
'Ye shall be unto me a kingdom of priests, and an holy nation.'
'Thou shalt have no other gods before me.'
'Honour thy father and thy mother.'
A command not to vex or oppress a stranger, for Israel were strangers in Egypt.
God promises to send an angel before Israel to keep them in the way.
The elders of Israel beheld God, and under His feet was a pavement of sapphire.
'Let them make me a sanctuary; that I may dwell among them.'
God promises to commune with Moses from above the mercy seat.
A golden plate engraved HOLINESS TO THE LORD is placed on Aaron's forehead.
'I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will be their God.'
Bezaleel is filled with the spirit of God in wisdom, understanding, and knowledge.
Moses offers to be blotted out of God's book if Israel is not forgiven.
'The LORD spake unto Moses face to face, as a man speaketh unto his friend.'
'My presence shall go with thee, and I will give thee rest.'
God proclaims Himself merciful and gracious, longsuffering, and abundant in goodness and truth.
Keeping mercy for thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin.
Moses blessed the people after seeing all the work done as the Lord commanded.
The glory of the LORD filled the tabernacle.
The cloud of the LORD was upon the tabernacle by day and fire by night throughout all their journeys.
